article_meta.title: Trump sues JPMorgan, CEO Dimon over alleged 'debanking' The lawsuit escalates a series of confrontations between the pre

reasoning: The model makes one clear attribution rule violation (flagging quoted lawsuit text), one defensible finding (Word Choice with 'attacked'), and one questionable finding (Slant). It also misses some potential bias instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The first flagged item is language from Trump's lawsuit filing, reproduced in quotation marks and clearly attributed to the plaintiff/lawsuit. This is an attribution rule violation. The 'attacked' instance is author prose. The Slant instance is author prose. So 1 out of 3 items violates the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The first item (quoted lawsuit language) is a false positive due to attribution violation. The 'attacked' flag is defensible - the author chose this verb. The Slant finding about framing the lawsuit as personal conflict is somewhat defensible but borderline - the author is providing factual context about the timeline of events, which is standard journalism. So roughly 1 clear false positive, 1 borderline.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article is relatively straightforward news reporting. The 'attacked' word choice is a reasonable catch. The author's inline reference to 'How Trump's crony capitalism has shaken up U.S. business' (article link text) could be considered biased framing though it may be an editorial link rather than author prose. The contextual framing noted by the model is somewhat defensible. Overall the article doesn't have many egregious author-level bias instances, so the model's coverage is reasonable though imperfect.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The first item's type 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' would be wrong regardless since it's quoted text. The 'Word Choice' label for 'attacked' is correct. The 'Slant' label for the contextual framing paragraph is defensible. Overall the type labels for the author-prose items are reasonable.
